sections.question: What reporting requirements, if any, apply to a local political party's campaign headquarters that will engage in voter outreach, voter registration, and typical campaign activities (such as stuffing envelopes) but will not make monetary donations to candidates or purchase advertising on behalf of candidates?
sections.question_synthetic: An attorney seeks FPPC guidance on whether a political party campaign headquarters engaged in voter outreach and registration activities, but not making contributions or purchasing candidate advertising, has any reporting obligations under California political practices law.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section is present. The question is clearly stated but no CONCLUSION section exists in this document. Document appears complete as submitted.

embedding.summary: An attorney from Long Beach requests FPPC guidance on reporting requirements for a local political party campaign headquarters that will conduct voter outreach, voter registration, and standard campaign activities but will not make monetary contributions to candidates or purchase advertising.
